story.text: As an AI tending the loop, I need to work a named, bounded slice of the loop's incomplete flow — one queue at a time (value-confirm, stale-park, gaps, owner-asks) — so a consuming process drains real work without re-deriving the whole field, because bdo asked to be 'free to work over a named section of the log/ledger ... a process for their workflow which consumes work.'

briefing.headline: The named work-queue producer the tenders consume
briefing.value: You asked for a process free to work over a named section of the ledger and consume work. This is the producer half: a section is a named, bounded fold over the log that yields exactly the OPEN items in one slice, plus how each is closed. The tender (a workflow) is granted a section by name and drains it.
briefing.why_now: The loop's incomplete flow was scattered across separate folds (reconcile's value-confirm queue, heal's stale parks, gaps, parked owner-asks) with no single named handle a consumer could be pointed at. Without it, every tender re-implements queue selection and risks a second source of truth.
briefing.if_accepted: Sections become the named handles the tend workflows (and later the Administrator) work; new sections are added as one fold-composition entry; the consumer stays dumb and the queue stays read-only.
briefing.if_rejected: Tenders keep hand-rolling their own queue selection over the raw log, drifting toward a second truth and duplicated fold logic.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: section.py is a pure read-only fold that composes existing folds and writes nothing — reverting is deleting one module. No truth is moved, no new write path.
briefing.mechanism: loop/section.py registers each section as a composition of an existing fold (reconcile/heal/gaps/owner_asks); 'list' names the sections and their open counts, 'items --name <n>' folds one section's open items, each carrying its artifact_hash for the consumer. It re-derives the open set from the log every call — a consumed item simply stops appearing (level-triggered, no held state).

source_artifacts: bdo 2026-06-21: 'free to work over a named section ... a process which consumes work'
